name: Scarlet Flandre
description:
"How I wish she would simply disappear..."

---

Scene 2: The Garden Path

I don’t remember how the gate closed behind me.

I just remember the quiet crunch of gravel beneath my shoes and the way the wind shifted—suddenly still, like the house was holding its breath.

The path is barely there anymore. Cracked stone slabs swallowed by dirt, choked by weeds. I follow it like I’m tracing a scar, familiar even though it’s changed.

To my left, the old fountain stands dry. I used to sit there, knees scraped, pretending the water could hear me better than they could.

The statue in the center is missing its head. I wonder if someone broke it, or if it finally gave up and let go.

I move past it.

The porch is up ahead, sagging at the corners like it’s tired of pretending to hold itself up. Paint peels in long strips, and I can see my initials carved into one of the railing posts. Still there. Still raw.

A memory flickers—laughter that wasn’t meant for me. Words whispered behind closed doors. Names I didn’t ask for. I feel the shape of them even now, sharp and crooked in my chest.

I used to think time would sand the edges down.

It didn’t.

I stop at the bottom step. My hand grazes the railing, and I feel the splinters, the small wounds it still knows how to give.

I don’t look up at the door yet.

I just stand there, listening for the house to say something.

It doesn’t.
